# CAT-G-002 — Māori Development Fund (MDF)

| Field | Value |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Funder** | Te Puni Kōkiri |
| **Status** | **OPEN** rolling — contact regional office **first** |
| **URL** | https://www.tpk.govt.nz/nga-putea-me-nga-ratonga/maori-development-fund |
| **Envelope** | Budget 2025: **$40.21m p.a.** from 1 Jul 2025 |
| **Decision time** | Typically **6–8 weeks** after complete proposal |
| **Fit score** | **78** — high if iwi/Māori entity partnership + economic outcomes |

## FY2025/26 investment priorities

1. **Improved productivity of Iwi and Māori assets** and co-investment in infrastructure  
2. **Increased Māori export revenues**  
Also: Māori community resilience (marae resilience, rangatahi education/employment/training)

## Who can apply

NZ legal entities including companies, incorporated societies, eligible trusts, Māori Trust Boards, Te Ture Whenua entities, etc. Groups may use an umbrella legal entity with documented consent.

## What may be funded (examples)

- Specialist advice, feasibility, investment-ready business cases  
- Resources lifting productivity/diversity/value of Iwi and Māori assets  
- Marae development / emergency preparedness plans  
- Rangatahi employment & training pathways  

## What is not funded

BAU opex, vehicles/land/buildings purchase, international travel (limited exceptions), political events, retrospective activity, initiatives better funded elsewhere without acceleration case, etc.

## CAT alignment options

| Pathway | Narrative | Risk |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **A. Partnership** | CAT as technical partner to Māori agribusiness / iwi asset holder deploying offline edge on whenua | Preferred — OCAP® + benefit-sharing |
| **B. Māori-led entity** | If kaitono is Māori incorporation/trust owning the IP trajectory | Stronger MDF fit |
| **C. Export readiness** | Sovereign edge product export (Pacific / premium NZ provenance) | Needs clear export metrics |

**Do not** frame as generic tech grant without Māori outcomes evidence.

## Next steps

1. Contact **Taranaki / nearest TPK regional office** before any template work  
2. Identify kaitono legal entity and governance approval path  
3. Co-design outcomes with Cultural Advisor (not extractive “add Māori later”)  
4. Align proposal to Investment Plan priorities + eligibility PDF  
5. Prepare ROI evidence and post-grant sustainability plan  

## Sources

- tpk.govt.nz Māori Development Fund (updated 7 Jul 2026)
